replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the 335i pricing across generations. The N54 and N55 have broadly similar replacement costs, though N54 engines (with twin turbos) may command a slight premium. Older generations: see the Era Map above for generation-specific pricing. A full engine replacement with labour can cost £9,000–£13,000 for premium vehicles.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a 335i engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £75–£110/hr (independent BMW specialist). N54 twin-turbo variants may require additional labour due to turbo system complexity.

problems.title: High‑Pressure Fuel Pump (HPFP) Failure — N54 (E90, 2006–2010)
problems.symptoms: Long cranking on cold or hot start, loss of power, engine stalling, engine warning light (limp mode), reduced fuel economy — in extreme cases, the car may leave you stranded.
problems.typicalMileage: 20,000–60,000 miles (some owners experienced multiple failures within a few thousand miles).

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you experience long cranking or stalling, book an inspection immediately.

problems.recommendation: HPFP failure is the most notorious issue with the N54 engine. BMW issued recalls covering over 150,000 vehicles in the US for N54 HPFP failures. Symptoms include extended cranking times, particularly when hot, and the car going into limp mode. A failed HPFP can leave you stranded. While replacement is straightforward, the N54's HPFP is a known weak point — budget for it. The N55 uses an updated HPFP design that is more reliable.

problems.title: Fuel Injector Failure — N54 (E90, 2006–2010)
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, misfire, hesitation on acceleration, engine warning light, fuel smell from the engine bay, increased fuel consumption.
problems.repairCost: £800–£2,000 [PLACEHOLDER] (injector replacement — all six injectors should ideally be replaced as a set with updated index 12 injectors).
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught early; ignored injector failure can damage the catalytic converter).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if misfire or rough running occurs.

problems.recommendation: The N54's piezo direct injectors are a known failure point. BMW released updated injectors (index 12) to address the issue, but failure can still occur on higher-mileage examples. Symptoms include rough idle, misfire, and fuel smell. When replacing injectors, it's strongly recommended to replace all six and have the DME calibrated. A full set replacement typically costs £800–£2,000 including labour — cheaper than dealing with a failed catalytic converter or engine damage.

problems.title: Wastegate Rattle / Turbocharger Failure — N54 (E90, 2006–2010)
problems.symptoms: Metallic rattling noise from the engine bay during deceleration or at idle, loss of boost pressure, reduced power, engine warning light.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught early; ignored turbo failure can send debris into the engine).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if you hear rattling or notice power loss.

problems.recommendation: The N54's twin turbochargers use wastegate actuators that can develop play over time, causing a characteristic rattle. While not an immediate failure, it indicates wear. BMW extended the warranty on N54 wastegates to 8 years/82,000 miles in some markets. On higher-mileage N54s, turbo replacement is a common expense. The N55's single twin-scroll turbo is more reliable in this regard.

problems.title: Oil Leaks — Valve Cover Gasket & Oil Filter Housing Gasket (N54 & N55)
problems.symptoms: Oil spots under the vehicle, burning oil smell, low oil level warning, visible oil leak from the top or front of the engine.
problems.repairCost: £300–£800 [PLACEHOLDER] (valve cover gasket or oil filter housing gasket replacement).
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught early; ignored oil loss can lead to engine damage).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if oil leak is detected.

problems.recommendation: Both N54 and N55 engines are known for oil leaks from the valve cover gasket and oil filter housing gasket (OFHG). The N55's valve cover is particularly prone to warping, requiring full cover replacement. If the OFHG fails, oil can leak onto the serpentine belt, causing it to slip or break — which can then be ingested into the engine through the front crankshaft seal, causing catastrophic failure. A £300–£800 gasket replacement is far cheaper than the consequences of a snapped belt.

problems.title: Water Pump & Thermostat Failure (N54 & N55)
problems.symptoms: Overheating, coolant loss, engine warning light, electric water pump noise, poor heater performance, the electric water pump may fail without warning.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught early; ignored overheating can cause head gasket failure).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if overheating or coolant loss occurs.

problems.recommendation: Both N54 and N55 use an electric water pump that is a known wear item. BMW's electric water pumps typically fail between 50,000–80,000 miles, often without warning. When it fails, the engine overheats rapidly — and in an aluminium-block engine, that can mean a warped head or blown head gasket. The thermostat often fails at the same time. A £500–£1,000 preventative replacement is cheaper than a £2,000+ head gasket repair.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW 3 Series 335i — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Worth fixing — a common and well-understood repair
Replace all six with updated index 12 injectors
Worth fixing on a well-maintained example
Full engine replacement often more cost‑effective

buyingChecks.title: Buying This BMW 3 Series 335i? Check These First
buyIf: * Full BMW service history with documented oil changes (every 8,000–10,000 miles)
* N54: HPFP replaced with updated part, injectors replaced with index 12 units
* N55: Valve cover and OFHG replaced, water pump replaced
* Evidence of premium fuel use (98 RON recommended for both engines)
avoidIf: * N54: Any sign of long cranking, misfire, or wastegate rattle
* N54: No evidence of HPFP or injector replacement on higher‑mileage cars
* N55: Any sign of oil leaks or overheating history
* No evidence of regular oil changes — both engines are intolerant of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The BMW 335i represents a pivotal moment in BMW history — the first mass‑production turbocharged petrol engine (N54) and its refined successor (N55). The N54 offers incredible tuning potential and a sonorous twin‑turbo character, but it comes with well‑documented weaknesses: HPFP, injectors, and turbos. The N55 is more reliable and responsive, with earlier torque delivery, but still suffers from oil leaks and water pump failures. Buy on service history above all else — a well‑maintained 335i of either engine is a rewarding and powerful car; a neglected example can be a financial black hole.

cta.label: → Compare 3 Series 335i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 3 Series 335i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: 1. Cold Start — Any rattle or wastegate noise? Any long cranking? (HPFP warning on N54)
2. Service History — Regular oil changes (8,000–10,000 miles) — this is non‑negotiable on both engines
3. HPFP / Injector History — N54: Have the HPFP and injectors been replaced with updated parts?
4. Oil Leaks — Check under the vehicle and around the valve cover and oil filter housing
5. Water Pump — Has the electric water pump been replaced? (N54 and N55)
6. Turbo Health — Any rattling, whistling, or loss of boost? (N54 wastegate rattle is common)
7. Running Quality — Any rough idle, hesitation, or misfire? (N54 injector warning)
8. MOT History — Check for emissions‑related failures or any advisory notes

items.label: SECTION 8 — FAQ
items.value: 1. How much does a BMW 3 Series 335i engine replacement cost in the UK?

items.label: A reconditioned 335i engine (N54 or N55) typically costs £2,500–£5,500 supply only depending on engine variant and condition. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,800. A full engine replacement with labour can cost £9,000–£13,000 for premium vehicles. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your vehicle.
items.value: 2. Is the BMW 3 Series 335i engine reliable?

items.label: It depends on which engine you have. The N54 (2006–2010) is a legendary engine with incredible tuning potential, but it has well‑documented weaknesses: HPFP failure, fuel injector failure, and wastegate rattle. The N55 (2010–2015) is more reliable, with a single twin‑scroll turbo and improved HPFP design, but still suffers from oil leaks and water pump failures. Both engines demand strict maintenance.
items.value: 3. What are the common problems with the BMW 3 Series 335i?

items.label: On the N54, the most common issues are HPFP failure, fuel injector failure, and wastegate rattle/turbocharger wear. On both N54 and N55, oil leaks (valve cover gasket, oil filter housing gasket) and electric water pump failure are common. The N55's valve cover is prone to warping, requiring full replacement.
items.value: 4. Is it worth replacing a 335i engine, or should I scrap the car?

items.label: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a tidy £5,000–£15,000 335i, a £2,800–£5,500 recon is 20–55% of the car's value — often worth it on well‑maintained examples. If the engine has suffered catastrophic failure (e.g., snapped timing chain, seized turbos), a full engine replacement may be necessary. Get a full diagnostic assessment before committing.
items.value: 5. How do I find my 3 Series's engine code?

items.label: The E90/E91/E92/E93 (2006–2010) uses the N54B30 twin‑turbo — look for two turbos under the bonnet. The E90 LCI (2010–2013) and F30/F31 (2011–2015) use the N55B30 twin‑scroll single turbo — look for one turbo with a twin‑scroll housing. The engine code is stamped on the engine block and listed on your V5C.
items.value: 6. Can I repair a 335i engine issue, or does it need replacement?

items.label: Most issues — HPFP failure, injector failure, oil leaks, water pump failure — are repairable. On the N54, if turbochargers fail, they can be replaced individually. However, if the engine suffers major internal failure (crankshaft, bearings, severe overheating), a full engine replacement is usually more cost‑effective than a rebuild.
items.value: Disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data from our network of engine specialists. Actual costs vary by variant, engine code, damage severity and workshop location. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes. Every engine supplied through EnginesMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited‑mileage warranty — always confirm terms with your chosen supplier.
